findings_text: Routine inspection of container from Hamilton. Declared steel coils - contents verified consistent. 24 coils matching bill of lading specifications. Weight verified within tolerance. Released for delivery.

Targeted inspection of container from US origin. Intelligence-led. X-ray revealed density anomaly. Physical exam found 8 undeclared cartons of counterfeit electronics concealed within declared machinery. Goods seized. Value $95K.
Random inspection of bulk cargo vessel carrying grain from Thunder Bay. Quality inspection per CFIA. Moisture content 12.8% (limit 14.5%). No infestation. Grade consistent with declaration. Released.
Referred inspection: weight discrepancy. Declared 15,400 kg, verified 18,200 kg. Difference 2,800 kg. Additional undeclared steel plate found. Importer fined $6,200. Amended documentation required.

Targeted pharmaceutical inspection. Reefer container from international transshipment. Temperature log verified continuous 4C. Health Canada Drug Licence confirmed. All documentation in order. Priority clearance.

Routine documentation audit of 8 containers from mixed US origins. One container flagged for HS code mismatch between declaration and commercial invoice. Potential duty underpayment $4,800. Held for clarification.

Intelligence-led inspection. Container from overseas transshipment. Full devanning. Contents declared as auto parts confirmed. However packaging inconsistencies noted on 2 pallets. Flagged for ongoing monitoring.
Radiation portal alarm for container. Secondary screening identified naturally occurring radioactive material in ceramic tile shipment. K-40 levels within limits. False alarm documented. Released.
Food safety inspection. Joint CBSA/CFIA. Canned goods from US. Labelling compliance check: 2 products missing bilingual labels per Consumer Packaging Act. Products held. Remaining cargo released.
Referred inspection based on trade analytics. Container with industrial chemicals as declared. Safety data sheets current. UN markings legible. TDG compliant. No issues found. Released.
Port State Control inspection of vessel. Crew certificates valid. Safety equipment current. One deficiency: emergency lighting in cargo hold below standard. 14-day correction deadline. No detention.

Random inspection of auto parts container. Contents consistent with declaration. Three cartons with undeclared promotional items of separate commercial value. Adjusted customs value. Additional duty $800.

Targeted underpayment review. Declared value significantly below market for commodity type. Physical exam confirmed higher-grade product. Value reassessed upward. Importer required to post $32,000 bond.
CFIA plant health inspection of wooden packaging from overseas. ISPM-15 compliance verified. Heat treatment stamps and IPPC marks present. No pest evidence. Released without conditions.
Personal effects examination for new resident from US. Household goods as declared. One restricted item (antique firearm) with valid import permit. Duty exemption confirmed for settler effects.

Targeted examination. Detector dog positive indication. Full devanning under controlled conditions. 12 packages of prohibited substance discovered. Total 8kg. Street value $1.2M. RCMP investigation. Two arrests at delivery.

Import compliance audit of consignee covering 90 days. 32 entries reviewed. Found consistent use of incorrect tariff for goods claiming CUSMA preferential rate without adequate origin documentation. Underpayment est. $41,000.
Joint CBSA/CFIA inspection of frozen meat from US. Temperature -20C verified. USDA export certificates validated. No signs of temperature abuse. Bilingual labelling confirmed. Released.
Vessel stores verification for outbound vessel. Declared stores consistent with physical count. All bonded stores properly sealed. No discrepancies. Clean inspection.

Routine courier shipment screening at Port Lands Terminal. 150 packages X-rayed. 5 flagged secondary. Results: 1 undeclared commercial quantity of supplements. 1 intellectual property concern. Remainder cleared. Compliance rate 98.7%.
Value anomaly flagged by analytics. Declared used equipment at $8K. Examination revealed near-new CNC machine valued at $165K. Significant undervaluation. Bond of $52,000 required.

Oversized project cargo inspection. Wind turbine blade from Hamilton. 65m length. Documentation verified. Engineering transport certificates confirmed. Import permit valid. Released for delivery to Ontario wind project.

Targeted inspection matching previous smuggling profile. Container from Great Lakes route. Full exam: aggregates as declared. All material tested genuine. No concealment detected. Lookout cleared for this shipment.
Post-release verification at importer warehouse. Goods classified under tariff 7208.51 (steel plate) verified correct. CUSMA origin documentation complete. Clean audit.
Expanded inspection of general cargo vessel. Deficiency found: cargo hold ventilation partially inoperable. Crew enclosed space entry records incomplete. Vessel detained 3 days until corrected.
